title: Container Health Check Failures
exclusions: Does not replace UC-3.1.13 restart-policy cadence analytics, UC-3.1.1 exit-code death taxonomy, UC-3.1.2 cgroup OOM kills, UC-3.1.3 CPU throttle diagnosis, UC-3.1.25 socket exposure, UC-3.1.6 privilege posture, or UC-3.1.28 Swarm replica convergence. Approved exclusions include containers with deliberately long HEALTHCHECK start-period for data warehouses or JVM cold starts, short-lived batch jobs that exit before probes matter, workloads monitored exclusively by Kubernetes kubelet probes without Docker HEALTHCHECK, and synthetic-only health programs documented in the service catalog with explicit waiver rows.
evidence: Saved search container_uc_3_1_22_health_check_failures; lookups health_check_baseline.csv and container_owner.csv versioned in git; weekly CSV exports to a restricted evidence index; dashboard drilldowns on docker:events health transitions, docker:inspect State.Health JSON, and docker:container_logs snippets. External research includes Honeycomb Kubernetes monitoring write-ups on correlating workload health signals, Kubernetes documentation on separating liveness, readiness, and startup probes to avoid probe-design anti-patterns, Docker reference material on health_status emission, and production incident literature where HTTP health checks masked partial outages until probe discipline improved.

controlTest.positiveScenario: On a lab Linux host run a container with HEALTHCHECK CMD curl -f http://127.0.0.1:wrong/health || exit 1 while the app serves on another port, ingest docker:events health_status transitions and docker:inspect State.Health rows, execute container_uc_3_1_22_health_check_failures, and expect a non-null severity among the six mandated tiers with populated container_id and probe context.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Run nginx:alpine with a correct HTTP HEALTHCHECK against the live listener, confirm predominantly healthy transitions, pass_rate_24h stays high in the search window, and verify the saved search emits no qualifying severity row across multiple five-minute intervals.

dataSources: index=oti_containers or index=containers sourcetype=docker:events filtered to Docker Engine health_status transitions (healthy, unhealthy, starting) equivalent to docker events --filter event=health_status; index=oti_containers or index=containers sourcetype=docker:inspect periodic JSON flattening State.Health.Status, State.Health.FailingStreak, State.Health.Log Output and ExitCode semantics (zero healthy, one unhealthy, two reserved), plus Config.Healthcheck.Test for probe_type classification and has_healthcheck detection; index=oti_containers or index=containers sourcetype=docker:container_logs application stdout and stderr for root-cause correlation within approximately sixty seconds of probe failure; governance join on health_check_baseline.csv via subsearch-wrapped inputlookup for per-image expected_pass_rate_24h_pct and production tier flags; optional Splunk DB Connect JDBC pulls for database reachability context when probe failures align with connection pool errors.
app: Splunk OpenTelemetry Collector for Docker when standardizing on OTLP export from Linux workers with HEC or OTLP endpoints into Splunk; customer-maintained Docker scripted inputs or Splunk Connect for Docker modular inputs streaming docker:events, plus privileged docker inspect exporters landing docker:inspect, and json-file or logging-driver followers for docker:container_logs; optional Splunk DB Connect for structured database status correlation alongside logs.
spl:
`comment("UC-3.1.22 Container Health Check Failures. Tunables: indexes (index=oti_containers OR index=containers); join inputlookup health_check_baseline.csv (image_key, expected_pass_rate_24h_pct, slo_tier, is_production) and container_owner.csv; docker:inspect poll cadence should be <=15m for restart delta fidelity; earliest=-24h latest=now.")`
| search (index=oti_containers OR index=containers) sourcetype="docker:events" earliest=-24h latest=now
| eval host_id=lower(toString(coalesce(host, Host, hostname, host_id, dest, "")))
| eval container_id=trim(toString(coalesce(container_id, containerId, id, Id, actor_id, actorId, "")))
| eval container_name=trim(toString(coalesce(container_name, containerName, name, Name, Actor__Attributes__name, "")))
| eval image=trim(toString(coalesce(Image, image, from, "")))
| eval lr=lower(_raw)
| eval action_lc=lower(toString(coalesce(action, Action, eventType, event_type, "")))
| where match(lr, "health_status") OR match(action_lc, "health_status")
| rex field=_raw "(?i)health_status:\\s*(?<hs_transition>healthy|unhealthy|starting)"
| eval hs2=lower(trim(toString(hs_transition)))
| rex field=lr "(?i)health_status:\\s*(?<hs_alt>healthy|unhealthy|starting)"
| eval hs_transition=lower(trim(coalesce(hs2, hs_alt,
    if(match(lr,"unhealthy") AND match(lr,"health_status"),"unhealthy",null()),
    if(match(lr,"healthy") AND match(lr,"health_status") AND NOT match(lr,"unhealthy"),"healthy",null()),
    if(match(lr,"starting") AND match(lr,"health_status"),"starting",null()))))
| where isnotnull(hs_transition) AND len(hs_transition)>0
| eval image_key=lower(trim(image))
| sort 0 host_id, container_id, _time
| streamstats window=400 current=t global=f last(eval(if(hs_transition=="healthy",_time,null()))) AS prev_healthy_ts BY host_id, container_id
| eval unhealthy_streak_min=if(hs_transition=="unhealthy" AND isnotnull(prev_healthy_ts), round((_time-prev_healthy_ts)/60, 2), null())
| eventstats count AS hs_events sum(eval(if(hs_transition=="healthy",1,0))) AS healthy_hits sum(eval(if(hs_transition=="unhealthy",1,0))) AS unhealthy_hits BY host_id, container_id, image
| eval denom=healthy_hits+unhealthy_hits
| eval pass_rate_24h=if(denom>0, round(100*healthy_hits/denom, 2), null())
| join type=left max=0 host_id, container_id [
    search (index=oti_containers OR index=containers) sourcetype="docker:inspect" earliest=-4h latest=now
    | eval host_id=lower(toString(coalesce(host, Host, hostname, host_id, dest, "")))
    | eval container_id=trim(toString(coalesce(container_id, Id, id, containerId, "")))
    | eval failing_streak=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(State__Health__FailingStreak, state_health_failing_streak, FailingStreak, failing_streak, "0")),10)
    | eval healthcfg=lower(toString(coalesce(Config__Healthcheck__Test, healthcheck_test, Healthcheck_Test, "")))
    | eval has_healthcheck=if(len(trim(healthcfg))>4,1,0)
    | eval probe_type=case(match(healthcfg,"curl"),"http_curl", match(healthcfg,"wget"),"http_wget", match(healthcfg,"pg_isready"),"postgres_ready", match(healthcfg,"redis-cli"),"redis_ping", match(healthcfg,"mysqladmin"),"mysql_ping", match(healthcfg,"nc |netcat"),"tcp_shell", true(),"other_or_shell")
    | eval restart_count=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(RestartCount, restart_count, "0")),10)
    | stats latest(failing_streak) AS failing_streak latest(has_healthcheck) AS has_healthcheck latest(probe_type) AS probe_type latest(restart_count) AS restart_count latest(image) AS image_inspect BY host_id, container_id
  ]
| join type=left max=0 host_id, container_id [
    search (index=oti_containers OR index=containers) sourcetype="docker:inspect" earliest=-24h latest=now
    | eval host_id=lower(toString(coalesce(host, Host, hostname, host_id, dest, "")))
    | eval container_id=trim(toString(coalesce(container_id, Id, id, containerId, "")))
    | eval rc=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(RestartCount, restart_count, "0")),10)
    | stats min(rc) AS rc_min max(rc) AS rc_max range(rc) AS rc_range BY host_id, container_id
  ]
| join type=left max=1 host_id, container_id [
    search (index=oti_containers OR index=containers) sourcetype="docker:container_logs" earliest=-24h latest=now
    | eval host_id=lower(toString(coalesce(host, Host, hostname, host_id, dest, "")))
    | eval container_id=trim(toString(coalesce(container_id, containerId, id, Id, "")))
    | eval lr2=lower(_raw)
    | eval root_cause_family=case(
        match(lr2,"(?i)timeout|timed\\s+out|deadline exceeded"),"downstream_timeout",
        match(lr2,"(?i)connection refused|econnrefused|refused\\s+connection"),"connection_refused",
        match(lr2,"(?i)out of memory|oom|cannot allocate|nospc|no space left"),"memory_or_disk_pressure",
        match(lr2,"(?i)panic|fatal error|segmentation fault"),"process_panic",
        match(lr2,"(?i)5\\d{2}\\b|internal server error|bad gateway|service unavailable"),"http_5xx_class",
        true(),"app_error_unclassified")
    | where match(lr2,"(?i)error|fatal|panic|refused|timeout|5\\d{2}|unavailable")
    | stats latest(root_cause_family) AS root_cause_family latest(_raw) AS log_evidence_snippet BY host_id, container_id
  ]
| join type=left max=0 image_key [
    | inputlookup health_check_baseline.csv
    | eval image_key=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(image_ref, image, Image, ""))))
    | eval expected_pass_rate_24h_pct=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(expected_pass_rate_24h_pct, baseline_pass_pct, golden_pass_pct, "")),10)
    | eval slo_tier=lower(toString(coalesce(slo_tier, tier, availability_tier, "")))
    | eval is_production=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(is_production, prod_flag, production_flag, "0")),10)
    | fields image_key expected_pass_rate_24h_pct slo_tier is_production
  ]
| eval prod_like=if(match(slo_tier,"(?i)prod|tier0|tier_0|mission|gold") OR is_production==1,1,0)
| eval unhealthy_no_restart=if((hs_transition=="unhealthy") AND (rc_max==rc_min) AND isnotnull(rc_max),1,0)
| eval severity=case(
    (isnotnull(unhealthy_streak_min)) AND unhealthy_streak_min>=15 AND prod_like==1, "critical_unhealthy_streak_above_15min_production",
    (has_healthcheck==0) AND prod_like==1, "critical_no_healthcheck_configured_production",
    unhealthy_no_restart==1 AND hs_transition=="unhealthy", "high_unhealthy_without_restart_silent_degradation",
    (isnotnull(pass_rate_24h)) AND pass_rate_24h<50 AND denom>=6, "high_pass_rate_below_50pct_24h",
    (isnotnull(failing_streak)) AND failing_streak>=3 AND hs_transition=="unhealthy", "medium_failing_streak_intermittent",
    (isnotnull(expected_pass_rate_24h_pct)) AND (isnotnull(pass_rate_24h)) AND pass_rate_24h<expected_pass_rate_24h_pct AND pass_rate_24h>=50, "low_baseline_pass_rate_drift",
    true(), null())
| where isnotnull(severity)
| eval recommended_response=case(
    severity=="critical_no_healthcheck_configured_production", "Add a Dockerfile HEALTHCHECK or Compose healthcheck, ship to registry, and block deploys without probe coverage on production-tier images; until then route synthetic checks to the real readiness surface.",
    severity=="critical_unhealthy_streak_above_15min_production", "Page service owner: treat as customer-facing degradation; pull docker inspect health logs, capture probe stdout, and fail traffic away at the load balancer until green; open incident with SLO impact.",
    severity=="high_unhealthy_without_restart_silent_degradation", "Silent degradation: restart policy may not recreate on unhealthy; force controlled recreate, validate dependency health, and compare against UC-3.1.13 restart telemetry for hidden backoff exhaustion.",
    severity=="high_pass_rate_below_50pct_24h", "Systemic probe instability: review probe command, timeouts, start-period, and dependency readiness; compare pass_rate_24h across image_key cohort and escalate if post-deploy regression.",
    severity=="medium_failing_streak_intermittent", "Intermittent probe failures: trend failing_streak with ingress 5xx; tune probe interval versus cold-cache effects and validate CPU throttle (UC-3.1.3) is not starving the probe.",
    severity=="low_baseline_pass_rate_drift", "Drift below health_check_baseline.csv expectation without crisis threshold: schedule image-owner review, validate baseline row freshness after golden image promotions.",
    true(), "Correlate docker:events health_status, docker:inspect State.Health, and docker:container_logs within sixty seconds before closing.")
| join type=left max=0 host_id, container_name [
    | inputlookup container_owner.csv
    | eval host_id=lower(toString(coalesce(host_id, host, Host, "")))
    | eval container_name=toString(container_name)
    | eval owner_team=toString(coalesce(owner_team, squad, ""))
    | fields host_id container_name owner_team
  ]
| table container_id container_name image host_id probe_type unhealthy_streak_min pass_rate_24h failing_streak root_cause_family severity recommended_response owner_team rc_range unhealthy_no_restart
description: This control monitors Docker HEALTHCHECK and Compose-defined health probe failures where the container process remains alive in dockerd yet repeatedly reports unhealthy, closing the silent-degradation window that restart-loop analytics deliberately ignore because dockerd does not restart on probe failure under common Compose and default restart policies. It correlates three telemetry feeds: Engine health_status transitions captured by docker events filtered to event=health_status for transition timing and unhealthy_streak_minutes math, docker:inspect snapshots that flatten State.Health.Status, State.Health.FailingStreak, the trailing Log entries with Output and ExitCode semantics where exit zero is healthy, exit one is unhealthy, and exit two is reserved, plus Config.Healthcheck.Test for probe_type classification and missing-HEALTHCHECK detection on production-tier images, and docker:container_logs lines streamed within roughly sixty seconds of an unhealthy transition to bucket root_cause_family from downstream_timeout, connection_refused, memory_or_disk_pressure, process_panic, http_5xx_class, or app_error_unclassified. The pipeline surfaces unhealthy_without_restart silent-degradation rows where RestartCount stays flat across the day, quantifies pass_rate_24h drift versus expected_pass_rate_24h_pct from health_check_baseline.csv, and complements UC-3.1.13 by catching workloads that fail probes without entering restart back-off, while remaining distinct from UC-3.1.1 exit-code death taxonomy, UC-3.1.2 cgroup OOM kills, and UC-3.1.3 CPU throttle diagnosis.
value: Quantifiable benefit appears as silent-degradation MTTR collapsing from hours to a single collection interval because the first page names unhealthy_streak_min, pass_rate_24h, root_cause_family, and the relevant container_id rather than asking on-call to grep five minutes of unstructured logs against a service that still passes naive uptime checks. Missing-HEALTHCHECK gap closure across the image registry becomes measurable when production-tier digests promote, which is the single highest-leverage hygiene change for SLO error-budget protection on dockerd-native estates that still rely on Docker rather than Kubernetes for production scheduling. Customer-visible HTTP 5xx episodes shrink because responders drain traffic at the load balancer while the process is still breathing, instead of waiting for a crash that never arrives. Fleet operators consolidate tooling on Splunk when probe transitions, inspect posture, and correlated error lines flow through one pipeline, eliminating per-cluster probe-only SaaS dashboards. Executive reviews finally see that observability covers application readiness, not only process existence, which is the conversation that closes audit findings on availability monitoring scope. FinOps benefits when probe-pattern bugs such as curl removed during apt-get clean in slim images, port mismatches between probe URL and listener, or aggressive intervals during CPU throttle windows surfaced by UC-3.1.3 are caught before fleet-wide rebuilds amplify retry storms.
implementation: Enable docker:events, docker:inspect, and docker:container_logs into index=oti_containers or index=containers with stable container_id keys, publish health_check_baseline.csv and container_owner.csv weekly, save container_uc_3_1_22_health_check_failures every five minutes on earliest=-24h latest=now, route critical severities to platform and owner_team jointly, archive weekly CSV snapshots to the evidence index, and keep the comment macro aligned with approved index names.
visualization: Heatmap unhealthy_streak_min by container_name across twenty-four hours; stacked bar chart pass_rate_24h by image with baseline reference line from health_check_baseline.csv; donut or treemap of root_cause_family for the trailing hour; severity-tiered table with cell coloring and drilldowns to inspect health logs and raw application errors.
cimSpl:
| tstats summariesonly=t latest(Application_State.state) AS st latest(Application_State.info) AS info FROM datamodel=Application_State WHERE nodename=Application_State earliest=-4h latest=now BY Application_State.dest Application_State.app
| rename Application_State.dest AS host_id
| where st!="running" OR like(lower(info),"%degrad%")
dataModelAcceleration: Accelerate Splunk CIM Application_State and Performance data models on the search tier when normalized service health overlays complement Engine telemetry; retain at least thirty days of summaries for quarterly SLO reviews that compare probe pass-rate trends to customer-facing error budgets.

references.title: Docker Docs — docker events CLI reference
Honeycomb — How Honeycomb monitors Kubernetes (probe and workload context)
Kubernetes Docs — Pod lifecycle: container probes
Splunk Lantern — Getting Docker log data into Splunk Cloud Platform with OpenTelemetry
OpenTelemetry — Semantic conventions: Container resource attributes

knownFalsePositives: Dependency cold-start races on large JVM, Spark, or warehouse images can flip unhealthy briefly while threads warm; pair with start-period tuning and health_check_baseline.csv annotations before paging. Coordinated database failovers emit connection refused lines that look like application bugs; correlate to DB change tickets and downstream restart windows. Probe misconfigurations such as curl against the wrong port or HTTP one versus HTTPS produce chronic unhealthy signals without true user impact until someone fixes the command; validate HEALTHCHECK against the real listener. Legitimate read-only maintenance modes may return HTTP 423 (Locked) on the probe path on purpose; use maintenance lookup flags rather than global suppression. Transient overlay or VPC path flaps during network maintenance can fail HTTP probes intermittently; require sustained streak thresholds on production tiers. Slim images that removed curl after a package cleanup cause probe exit two or executable missing errors; distinguish toolchain failure from app failure before blaming service owners. Aggressive probe intervals on CPU-starved hosts (see UC-3.1.3) can time out under throttle even when dependency health is fine; compare cgroup cpu.stat before opening sev-one bridges. Blue-green cuts that intentionally keep old tasks unhealthy while draining can resemble silent degradation unless deploy ids label the event stream. CI ephemeral runners with toy HEALTHCHECK examples may pollute non-prod indexes unless routed separately.

premiumApps.name: Splunk ITSI
premiumApps.note: optional — model probe pass-rate KPIs per docker host entity and episodes when silent degradation aligns with dependent business services

detailedImplementation:
Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform owns this control together with the service-reliability lead and the observability engineer who certifies Docker telemetry on Linux worker fleets. UC-3.1.22 closes the silent-degradation gap where a container process remains alive, docker ps still shows running, and restart-focused analytics such as UC-3.1.13 may stay quiet because dockerd does not treat HEALTHCHECK failure as a restart trigger under common Compose and default restart policies. UC-3.1.1 still matters for exit-code taxonomy when the main process dies; this use case matters when the main process lives but the application surface is wrong for customers. Before you schedule the saved search, confirm three writers exist in Splunk: docker:events with Docker Engine health_status transitions (equivalent to docker events filtered to health_status), docker:inspect snapshots that flatten State.Health.Status, State.Health.FailingStreak, State.Health.Log exit codes plus Config.Healthcheck.Test, and docker:container_logs for stderr and stdout tails keyed to container identity. Splunk OpenTelemetry Collector for Docker is the preferred long-term pipeline when your enterprise standardizes on OTLP export from the node, provided you map semantic attributes for container.id and container.name consistently with daemon-side scripted inputs. Where teams still operate classic forwarders, privileged scripted inputs that call the Engine API or stream docker events into HEC satisfy the same contract. Optional Splunk DB Connect jobs help when probe failures correlate with database reachability and you want JDBC evidence beside container logs; treat DB Connect as enrichment, not a replacement for Engine health events.

Governance lookups sit beside the saved search. Publish health_check_baseline.csv with columns image_key (lowercase registry or repo string aligned to Config.Image), expected_pass_rate_24h_pct (golden probe pass percentage over a day), slo_tier or availability_tier, and is_production or prod_flag as one when the image is in a catalog tier that must never ship without a probe. Refresh the CSV whenever golden images promote. Maintain container_owner.csv with host_id, container_name, owner_team for paging parity with sibling UC-3.1.x searches. If you already operate container_inspect.csv for UC-3.1.13, reuse its RestartCount cadence expectations so reviewers see consistent inspect freshness. Roles must allow search on index=oti_containers or index=containers per your security office naming standard, and restrict docker:inspect indexes to platform engineers because flattened health logs can echo internal URLs.

Risk framing for incident commanders: load balancers and ingress controllers often drain tasks only after TCP failures or explicit readiness changes. A workload can answer SYN while returning HTTP 503 on the application port, and a naive docker ps dashboard still looks green. HEALTHCHECK encodes an application-level contract; when it flips unhealthy without a restart, revenue-impacting errors can accumulate while paging systems wait for a crash that never arrives. This control therefore prioritizes correlation across events, inspect, and logs rather than any single metric.

Differentiation recap relative to sibling UCs: UC-3.1.13 measures restart-policy cadence and backoff exhaustion; it does not require probe state. UC-3.1.1 classifies die events and exit codes. UC-3.1.2 reads cgroup memory kills. UC-3.1.3 surfaces CPU throttle before probes starve. UC-3.1.25 and UC-3.1.6 cover security posture, not availability semantics. UC-3.1.28 answers Swarm replica convergence. Kubernetes probe narratives belong under UC-3.2.x, yet operators still mirror liveness versus readiness discipline when authoring Docker HEALTHCHECK commands, which is why runbooks cite kube documentation for pattern hygiene.

Licensing and volume: docker events for health_status are low volume compared to full json-file log scraping, but docker:container_logs can dominate license when services log verbosely after each probe failure. Consider routing high-chatter debug services to shorter retention indexes until owners fix log storms triggered by dependency timeouts.

Legal and privacy: State.Health.Log Output fields may contain SQL fragments or internal hostnames. Redact at collection when possible and restrict dashboard roles.

Step 2 — Configure data collection

On every Linux worker running Docker CE, Mirantis Container Runtime, or an approved enterprise Engine build, enable a collector path that preserves health_status events. When using Splunk Connect for Docker or an equivalent modular input, confirm the Docker Events stream is not filtered in a way that drops health_status. Validate with docker events on the host and compare _time skew to Splunk within thirty seconds. Normalize host_id to the lowercase short hostname Universal Forwarders emit so joins to container_owner.csv stay deterministic across fleets that mix FQDN and short names in legacy data.

Schedule docker:inspect ingestion at fifteen minutes or faster on production workers because restart_count deltas in the SPL infer silent degradation only when multiple snapshots exist in the trailing day. The scripted input should flatten camelCase and snake_case variants for State.Health and Config.Healthcheck because different exporter versions rename keys. Include container_id, Name, Config.Image, RestartCount, and the latest five health log elements when your exporter can retain them; at minimum capture Status, FailingStreak, and the most recent ExitCode. Where JSON payloads grow large, strip unrelated Mounts arrays at the forwarder after health extraction to save license.

For docker:container_logs, map sourcetype docker:container_logs and preserve container_id fields extracted from json-file metadata or the logging driver envelope. If Compose project prefixes alter display names, document whether container_name keys align with docker:events Actor attributes or with inspect Name so joins do not miss during incidents.

When standardizing on Splunk OpenTelemetry Collector for Docker, configure receivers that capture container logs and map resource attributes to the same container_id strings docker inspect uses. Align OTLP semantic conventions for container attributes with internal CMDB keys. HEC tokens require vault storage and quarterly rotation.

Expected validation searches before alert authoring: index filter with sourcetype=docker:events and raw text health_status, sourcetype=docker:inspect with State__Health__Status populated for a canary container, and sourcetype=docker:container_logs with error lines during a deliberate probe failure drill. Clock skew beyond one minute breaks streamstats ordering; enforce NTP.

Step 3 — Create search and alert

Save the SPL as saved search container_uc_3_1_22_health_check_failures with schedule every five minutes (or fifteen minutes when Job Inspector shows scan pressure) and time range earliest=-24h latest=now during steady state. Throttle duplicate pages per host_id and container_id for thirty minutes on medium and low tiers while allowing immediate re-fire when severity escalates between tiers within the same hour. Route critical strings to platform and owner_team jointly; attach log_evidence_snippet when present.

Understanding the pipeline in operator terms: the opening comment macro records indexes, lookup owners, inspect cadence expectations, and the explicit differentiation from restart-only analytics. The primary arm reads docker:events health transitions with coalesce-friendly field lists for mixed TA versions. streamstats computes minutes since the last healthy transition per container for unhealthy rows. eventstats rolls pass_rate_24h from healthy versus unhealthy counts in the window. Two inspect joins layer current failing_streak, probe_type classification, has_healthcheck, and restart_count range for silent-degradation inference. A docker:container_logs join supplies root_cause_family buckets from typical error signatures. The health_check_baseline.csv join carries expected_pass_rate_24h_pct and production tier flags without using a standalone lookup command on the main search. The severity case emits only the six mandated tier strings. recommended_response gives paging-bridge text. The closing container_owner.csv join routes owner_team.

Alert actions should link to your load balancer or ingress health view for the same service, include the table row, and deep link to UC-3.1.13 when restart backoff questions arise.

Fenced SPL for runbooks must match the spl JSON field byte-for-byte:

```spl
`comment("UC-3.1.22 Container Health Check Failures. Tunables: indexes (index=oti_containers OR index=containers); join inputlookup health_check_baseline.csv (image_key, expected_pass_rate_24h_pct, slo_tier, is_production) and container_owner.csv; docker:inspect poll cadence should be <=15m for restart delta fidelity; earliest=-24h latest=now.")`
| search (index=oti_containers OR index=containers) sourcetype="docker:events" earliest=-24h latest=now
| eval host_id=lower(toString(coalesce(host, Host, hostname, host_id, dest, "")))
| eval container_id=trim(toString(coalesce(container_id, containerId, id, Id, actor_id, actorId, "")))
| eval container_name=trim(toString(coalesce(container_name, containerName, name, Name, Actor__Attributes__name, "")))
| eval image=trim(toString(coalesce(Image, image, from, "")))
| eval lr=lower(_raw)
| eval action_lc=lower(toString(coalesce(action, Action, eventType, event_type, "")))
| where match(lr, "health_status") OR match(action_lc, "health_status")
| rex field=_raw "(?i)health_status:\\s*(?<hs_transition>healthy|unhealthy|starting)"
| eval hs2=lower(trim(toString(hs_transition)))
| rex field=lr "(?i)health_status:\\s*(?<hs_alt>healthy|unhealthy|starting)"
| eval hs_transition=lower(trim(coalesce(hs2, hs_alt,
    if(match(lr,"unhealthy") AND match(lr,"health_status"),"unhealthy",null()),
    if(match(lr,"healthy") AND match(lr,"health_status") AND NOT match(lr,"unhealthy"),"healthy",null()),
    if(match(lr,"starting") AND match(lr,"health_status"),"starting",null()))))
| where isnotnull(hs_transition) AND len(hs_transition)>0
| eval image_key=lower(trim(image))
| sort 0 host_id, container_id, _time
| streamstats window=400 current=t global=f last(eval(if(hs_transition=="healthy",_time,null()))) AS prev_healthy_ts BY host_id, container_id
| eval unhealthy_streak_min=if(hs_transition=="unhealthy" AND isnotnull(prev_healthy_ts), round((_time-prev_healthy_ts)/60, 2), null())
| eventstats count AS hs_events sum(eval(if(hs_transition=="healthy",1,0))) AS healthy_hits sum(eval(if(hs_transition=="unhealthy",1,0))) AS unhealthy_hits BY host_id, container_id, image
| eval denom=healthy_hits+unhealthy_hits
| eval pass_rate_24h=if(denom>0, round(100*healthy_hits/denom, 2), null())
| join type=left max=0 host_id, container_id [
    search (index=oti_containers OR index=containers) sourcetype="docker:inspect" earliest=-4h latest=now
    | eval host_id=lower(toString(coalesce(host, Host, hostname, host_id, dest, "")))
    | eval container_id=trim(toString(coalesce(container_id, Id, id, containerId, "")))
    | eval failing_streak=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(State__Health__FailingStreak, state_health_failing_streak, FailingStreak, failing_streak, "0")),10)
    | eval healthcfg=lower(toString(coalesce(Config__Healthcheck__Test, healthcheck_test, Healthcheck_Test, "")))
    | eval has_healthcheck=if(len(trim(healthcfg))>4,1,0)
    | eval probe_type=case(match(healthcfg,"curl"),"http_curl", match(healthcfg,"wget"),"http_wget", match(healthcfg,"pg_isready"),"postgres_ready", match(healthcfg,"redis-cli"),"redis_ping", match(healthcfg,"mysqladmin"),"mysql_ping", match(healthcfg,"nc |netcat"),"tcp_shell", true(),"other_or_shell")
    | eval restart_count=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(RestartCount, restart_count, "0")),10)
    | stats latest(failing_streak) AS failing_streak latest(has_healthcheck) AS has_healthcheck latest(probe_type) AS probe_type latest(restart_count) AS restart_count latest(image) AS image_inspect BY host_id, container_id
  ]
| join type=left max=0 host_id, container_id [
    search (index=oti_containers OR index=containers) sourcetype="docker:inspect" earliest=-24h latest=now
    | eval host_id=lower(toString(coalesce(host, Host, hostname, host_id, dest, "")))
    | eval container_id=trim(toString(coalesce(container_id, Id, id, containerId, "")))
    | eval rc=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(RestartCount, restart_count, "0")),10)
    | stats min(rc) AS rc_min max(rc) AS rc_max range(rc) AS rc_range BY host_id, container_id
  ]
| join type=left max=1 host_id, container_id [
    search (index=oti_containers OR index=containers) sourcetype="docker:container_logs" earliest=-24h latest=now
    | eval host_id=lower(toString(coalesce(host, Host, hostname, host_id, dest, "")))
    | eval container_id=trim(toString(coalesce(container_id, containerId, id, Id, "")))
    | eval lr2=lower(_raw)
    | eval root_cause_family=case(
        match(lr2,"(?i)timeout|timed\\s+out|deadline exceeded"),"downstream_timeout",
        match(lr2,"(?i)connection refused|econnrefused|refused\\s+connection"),"connection_refused",
        match(lr2,"(?i)out of memory|oom|cannot allocate|nospc|no space left"),"memory_or_disk_pressure",
        match(lr2,"(?i)panic|fatal error|segmentation fault"),"process_panic",
        match(lr2,"(?i)5\\d{2}\\b|internal server error|bad gateway|service unavailable"),"http_5xx_class",
        true(),"app_error_unclassified")
    | where match(lr2,"(?i)error|fatal|panic|refused|timeout|5\\d{2}|unavailable")
    | stats latest(root_cause_family) AS root_cause_family latest(_raw) AS log_evidence_snippet BY host_id, container_id
  ]
| join type=left max=0 image_key [
    | inputlookup health_check_baseline.csv
    | eval image_key=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(image_ref, image, Image, ""))))
    | eval expected_pass_rate_24h_pct=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(expected_pass_rate_24h_pct, baseline_pass_pct, golden_pass_pct, "")),10)
    | eval slo_tier=lower(toString(coalesce(slo_tier, tier, availability_tier, "")))
    | eval is_production=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(is_production, prod_flag, production_flag, "0")),10)
    | fields image_key expected_pass_rate_24h_pct slo_tier is_production
  ]
| eval prod_like=if(match(slo_tier,"(?i)prod|tier0|tier_0|mission|gold") OR is_production==1,1,0)
| eval unhealthy_no_restart=if((hs_transition=="unhealthy") AND (rc_max==rc_min) AND isnotnull(rc_max),1,0)
| eval severity=case(
    (isnotnull(unhealthy_streak_min)) AND unhealthy_streak_min>=15 AND prod_like==1, "critical_unhealthy_streak_above_15min_production",
    (has_healthcheck==0) AND prod_like==1, "critical_no_healthcheck_configured_production",
    unhealthy_no_restart==1 AND hs_transition=="unhealthy", "high_unhealthy_without_restart_silent_degradation",
    (isnotnull(pass_rate_24h)) AND pass_rate_24h<50 AND denom>=6, "high_pass_rate_below_50pct_24h",
    (isnotnull(failing_streak)) AND failing_streak>=3 AND hs_transition=="unhealthy", "medium_failing_streak_intermittent",
    (isnotnull(expected_pass_rate_24h_pct)) AND (isnotnull(pass_rate_24h)) AND pass_rate_24h<expected_pass_rate_24h_pct AND pass_rate_24h>=50, "low_baseline_pass_rate_drift",
    true(), null())
| where isnotnull(severity)
| eval recommended_response=case(
    severity=="critical_no_healthcheck_configured_production", "Add a Dockerfile HEALTHCHECK or Compose healthcheck, ship to registry, and block deploys without probe coverage on production-tier images; until then route synthetic checks to the real readiness surface.",
    severity=="critical_unhealthy_streak_above_15min_production", "Page service owner: treat as customer-facing degradation; pull docker inspect health logs, capture probe stdout, and fail traffic away at the load balancer until green; open incident with SLO impact.",
    severity=="high_unhealthy_without_restart_silent_degradation", "Silent degradation: restart policy may not recreate on unhealthy; force controlled recreate, validate dependency health, and compare against UC-3.1.13 restart telemetry for hidden backoff exhaustion.",
    severity=="high_pass_rate_below_50pct_24h", "Systemic probe instability: review probe command, timeouts, start-period, and dependency readiness; compare pass_rate_24h across image_key cohort and escalate if post-deploy regression.",
    severity=="medium_failing_streak_intermittent", "Intermittent probe failures: trend failing_streak with ingress 5xx; tune probe interval versus cold-cache effects and validate CPU throttle (UC-3.1.3) is not starving the probe.",
    severity=="low_baseline_pass_rate_drift", "Drift below health_check_baseline.csv expectation without crisis threshold: schedule image-owner review, validate baseline row freshness after golden image promotions.",
    true(), "Correlate docker:events health_status, docker:inspect State.Health, and docker:container_logs within sixty seconds before closing.")
| join type=left max=0 host_id, container_name [
    | inputlookup container_owner.csv
    | eval host_id=lower(toString(coalesce(host_id, host, Host, "")))
    | eval container_name=toString(container_name)
    | eval owner_team=toString(coalesce(owner_team, squad, ""))
    | fields host_id container_name owner_team
  ]
| table container_id container_name image host_id probe_type unhealthy_streak_min pass_rate_24h failing_streak root_cause_family severity recommended_response owner_team rc_range unhealthy_no_restart
```

Step 4 — Validate

Positive path A — deliberate unhealthy probe: run a lab container with HEALTHCHECK CMD curl -f http://127.0.0.1:9999/health || exit 1 while the app listens on a different port, wait for health_status transitions, and confirm docker:events rows appear with unhealthy while the process stays up. Execute the saved search and expect a non-null severity tier with failing_streak or pass_rate context.

Positive path B — inspect alignment: docker inspect --format '{{json .State.Health}}' must match State.Health fields visible in Splunk after normalization. Mismatches indicate props gaps rather than benign noise.

Positive path C — silent degradation: choose restart policy no or unless-stopped without Swarm health overrides, force repeated probe failures, verify RestartCount stays flat while State.Health.Status reads unhealthy, and confirm high_unhealthy_without_restart_silent_degradation or equivalent tier when rc_range stays zero across inspect polls.

Positive path D — baseline drift: load health_check_baseline.csv with an expected_pass_rate_24h_pct above observed healthy ratio in lab, confirm low_baseline_pass_rate_drift when pass_rate_24h remains at or above fifty but below expectation.

Negative path — healthy nginx: run nginx:alpine with a correct HTTP HEALTHCHECK, confirm healthy transitions dominate, pass_rate_24h stays high, and the search emits no qualifying severity row across several intervals.

Field sanity: temporarily rename TA fields in a sandbox forwarder to mimic camelCase-only payloads and confirm coalesce still extracts hs_transition. RBAC: readers without the containers index must see zero rows. Correlation: compare alert times to synthetic checks and ingress 5xx dashboards; misalignment often means probe endpoints diverge from customer paths.

Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

Case A — Production container unhealthy longer than fifteen minutes without restart: treat as SLO risk; drain traffic, capture inspect health logs, validate dependencies, then recreate under change control. Compare timeline to UC-3.1.13 for hidden restart exhaustion.

Case B — Missing HEALTHCHECK on a production-tier golden image: block pipeline promotion, open backlog item to add Dockerfile HEALTHCHECK or Compose healthcheck with realistic start-period, and route temporary coverage via synthetic checks documented in the service catalog.

Case C — Probe pattern mismatch such as curl against port eighty while the app listens on eight thousand and eight: fix HEALTHCHECK command and document port contract in image readme; verify curl exists in slim images.

Case D — Cold-start grace race on legitimate deploy: JVM or data warehouse warm-up exceeds start-period; raise start-period and retries in YAML, annotate health_check_baseline.csv for long-start class, and avoid muting the UC globally.

Case E — Downstream database restart cascade: logs show connection refused bursts; pivot to database operational runbooks, temporarily widen probe timeout only with owner approval, and correlate DB Connect rows if enabled.

Case F — Probe binary missing after image slimming: inspect health log shows executable not found; rebuild image with curl or wget, or switch to a built-in application probe command.

Case G — Planned maintenance read-only mode: application deliberately returns HTTP 423 (Locked) on the probe path during a maintenance window; add a maintenance window lookup flag or expected_fail_window column to health_check_baseline.csv rather than silencing the search globally.

Case H — Transient overlay network blips during cluster network reconfiguration: short unhealthy spikes without log anomalies; require sustained streak or failing_streak thresholds before pages in non-prod tiers.

Case I — streamstats streak wrong after bulk replay: deduplicate replayed events or add _indextime tie-breakers in a diagnostic clone when _time duplicates appear.

Case J — join misses on container_owner: normalize Compose project prefixes between CSV publishers and event fields; add alias rows instead of loosening join keys.

Dashboard publishing: heatmap unhealthy_streak_min by container_name, bar chart pass_rate_24h by image, donut root_cause_family for the latest hour, and a severity-tier table with drilldowns to raw events, inspect JSON, and log snippets.

Evidence retention: weekly CSV exports of the alert table with lookup commit hashes satisfy internal audit samples when paired with change tickets that document HEALTHCHECK edits.

Governance: quarterly replay one historical incident through the SPL after Engine upgrades, and update the comment macro when indexes move.

Closing checklist: five step headers use plain text with em dashes; Step 3 fenced SPL matches the spl field exactly; monitoringType lists Reliability and Availability; cimModels include Application_State; equipment lists docker only; equipmentModels include docker_engine and docker_compose; narrative JSON fields contain no asterisk emphasis; knownFalsePositives stay reliability-themed; references include Docker HEALTHCHECK, docker events, an external engineering article, Kubernetes probe documentation, a specific Splunk Lantern article path, and OpenTelemetry container semantic conventions; no forbidden boilerplate phrases from the gold contract list appear in narrative fields.

Supplemental engineering notes for long-term owners: when rootless Docker changes socket paths, revalidate event inputs before trusting streak math. When migrating to Kubernetes-only nodes, retire or fork this UC for dockerd-free classes. When finance challenges ingest cost, compare license bytes to revenue risk of silent HTTP 5xx storms during peak business hours. When legal requests holds, include inspect health logs and correlated application logs in preservation scope. When service meshes add sidecars, ensure health probes target the application container port, not the mesh admin port. When automating remediation, require human approval before fleet-wide recreate in regulated zones. When training new responders, teach the difference between ENGINE health_status events and external synthetic monitors. When Splunk Cloud search autoscaling changes, revalidate schedule overlap. When closing incidents, record whether the fix was probe design, dependency recovery, resource limits, or traffic drain. When handling zonal outages, use region macros tied to baseline tiers. When reviewing Mirantis support bundles, compare their health output to your flattened inspect fields. When OT edge gateways run Docker, duplicate baselines with OT-specific slo_tier values.

Appendix — Field contract for health_check_baseline.csv

image_key must match lower(trim(Config.Image)) strings your inspect exporter emits, including registry host when that is how developers pin images. expected_pass_rate_24h_pct should reflect rolling acceptance criteria after dependencies are warm, not greenfield lab numbers. slo_tier values prod, tier0, mission, and gold set prod_like in SPL; extend the match list only through CAB records. is_production accepts one or zero when your CMDB cannot express tier strings. When images serve multiple environments, duplicate rows with distinct suffix keys or maintain parallel CSV files per estate with macro-selected filenames documented in the comment macro.

Appendix — Probe taxonomy expectations

http_curl and http_wget buckets assume HEALTHCHECK uses those binaries; postgres_ready maps pg_isready, redis_ping maps redis-cli PING patterns, mysql_ping maps mysqladmin ping, tcp_shell maps nc or netcat style probes, and other_or_shell captures bespoke scripts that still merit review. When teams embed GRPC health, classify as other_or_shell until you add a dedicated match branch in a wrapper macro.

Appendix — Correlation window discipline

The docker:container_logs join in the baseline SPL uses a twenty-four hour window for practicality in paste-and-run deployments. Tighten the subsearch to earliest=-5m latest=+1m relative to each health_status event in production by wrapping a transaction or map command inside a saved-search macro once performance testing completes. Document the tighter macro name beside the saved search description.

Appendix — Silent degradation and restart counters

restart_count range relies on inspect polls. Single-sample days produce rc_range zero for benign reasons; require minimum poll count per host class or compare against UC-3.1.13 inspect export cadence before trusting unhealthy_no_restart alone. When in doubt, open docker events for die and restart actions in parallel.

Appendix — FinOps alignment

Attach pass_rate_24h trends to image promotion tickets so finance sees monitoring maturity moving with release velocity. Pair weekly exports with customer SLO review decks that cite p95 error budget burn during probe failure windows.

Appendix — Security alignment

Restrict dashboards that expose HEALTHCHECK commands because they may reveal internal admin paths. Mask log_evidence_snippet in tier-one analyst views when raw logs contain personally identifiable information.

Appendix — Performance alignment

If multisearch or join costs grow, summarize docker:events health transitions into fifteen-minute metrics indexes retaining healthy and unhealthy counts per container before alerting.

Appendix — Training alignment

Run tabletop exercises where operators must decide between raising start-period versus fixing dependency latency using the same telemetry this UC surfaces, preventing guesswork during incidents.

Appendix — Documentation alignment

Maintain an internal wiki page mapping TA field renames per Splunk Connect for Docker release so coalesce lists in SPL do not sprawl without review.

Appendix — Review cadence

Quarterly, replay one historical probe outage through the SPL after Docker Engine or Compose upgrades, and confirm health_status strings remain compatible with rex patterns.

Appendix — Escalation alignment

When severity is high_unhealthy_without_restart_silent_degradation, escalate if ingress HTTP 5xx share rises even when process uptime remains high, because that pattern often indicates customer-visible brownout.

Appendix — Telemetry hygiene

Deduplicate OpenTelemetry and legacy forwarder paths during migration using explicit source weights so pass_rate_24h does not double-count transitions.

Appendix — Collector hygiene

Cap docker:container_logs cardinality on CI executors or exclude builder profiles via macros to preserve search performance.

Appendix — Governance alignment

Require lookup owners to approve changes to expected_pass_rate_24h_pct in the same change record as Dockerfile HEALTHCHECK edits affecting the same image_key.

Final reminder: Kubernetes-native estates should pair this UC with UC-3.2.x probe analytics rather than assuming dockerd events exist on every node class.
grandmaExplanation: We watch the container equivalent of a doctor checking whether someone can really eat and keep food down, not just whether they are still walking. A box can look running while it serves bad answers for hours; this control notices that kind of quiet failure before customers absorb all the pain.
hardware: Targets sixty-four-bit Linux workers running Docker CE, Mirantis Container Runtime, or enterprise Engine builds on fleet SKUs such as Dell PowerEdge R750, HPE ProLiant DL360 Gen11, Lenovo ThinkSystem SR650, and AWS Graviton m7g families where health_status events and inspect JSON match upstream Docker semantics. Accurate streak and restart inference requires stable time sync on forwarders and managers, because streamstats ordering breaks when _time drifts. ARM and x86 differ only in image availability and cgroup paths, not in health_status strings. Windows containers and dockerd-free Kubernetes nodes without Engine events are out of scope for the default SPL without forked collection.
